title: Python rewrite: export monthly command
description: Port export-monthly.sh + lib/export_transform.sh (the most complex bash piece: range bounding, monthly bucketing, thread nesting, sealed-month idempotency guard, display_name resolution, file projection, noise stripping). Must reuse scripts/test_fixtures/export-archive/ fixtures and replicate every test_export_monthly.sh scenario exactly (nesting, cross-month threads, range bounding, idempotency/sealing, late-reply-reopens-sealed-month, seal-stamp predicate).
close_reason: Implemented export_logic.py (full port of export_transform.sh's range bounding, monthly bucketing, thread nesting, display_name resolution, file projection, noise stripping, sealed-month idempotency with late-reply reopening) and export.py CLI orchestration (convert -f export -> transform, matching export-monthly.sh's archive location/error handling exactly). 15 new tests in test_export_logic.py replicate every scenario from test_export_monthly.sh against the identical fixtures (72 total passing). Verified live: exported all 7 registered all-* channels across 4 workspaces, correct empty/wrote reporting, all-pax's 0-message archive handled gracefully with no crash.

title: Python rewrite: backup commands (backup channel / backup run)
description: Port backup.sh + run-backups.sh into backup.py. backup channel <id> <slug> <ws> <archive-root> [-f]: archive if no slackdump.sqlite exists yet, resume otherwise (never -dedupe, confirmed data-loss bug). backup run <channels.json> <archive-root> [-f]: validate, warm fast-tier catalog cache per distinct workspace, then back up each channel, continuing on per-channel failure (partial-failure exit code).
close_reason: Implemented backup_logic.py (backup_channel: archive-if-new/resume-if-existing/full-resync-into-fresh-dated-dir; run: validate channels.json, warm fast-tier catalog per distinct workspace, continue on per-channel failure). Wired into backup.py CLI. 7 new passing tests (57 total).

title: Python rewrite: workspace/channel/catalog commands
description: Port register-workspace.sh, register-channel.sh, validate-channels.sh, channel_catalog.sh, and catalog-channels.sh into the slackbackup CLI. New modules: slackdump.py (subprocess wrapper around the slackdump binary - workspace select/list/import, list channels JSON, search files/messages, archive/resume), catalog_logic.py (per-workspace catalog cache: fast/full tier merge rules, -no-chan-cache fix preserved, description via topic/purpose), channel_logic.py (channels.json validate/register/list, catalog-backed name lookup with full-tier fallback), workspace_logic.py (tokens file lookup, workspace import, status listing). Wire into workspace.py/channel.py/catalog.py CLI handlers. Must preserve all bug fixes already found in the bash version: -no-chan-cache (stale cross-workspace cache bug), full-tier-never-overwrites-member=yes, description-no-extra-API-call.
close_reason: Implemented and verified live: slackdump.py (subprocess wrapper, preserves -no-chan-cache fix and -no-json), catalog_logic.py (JSON-native cache replacing the bash TSV+marker-files approach, same fast/full merge rules), channel_logic.py, workspace_logic.py. Wired into workspace.py/channel.py/catalog.py CLI handlers (workspace register/list, channel register/list/validate, catalog show). 50 passing tests (36 new in this stage). Verified live: catalog show output matches the bash version exactly for f3pugetsound; channel register works end-to-end (lookup -> append -> idempotent re-run); workspace list matches register-workspace.sh's status output.

title: Python rewrite: project scaffold (pyproject.toml, venv, CLI skeleton)
description: Set up src/slackbackup package, pyproject.toml (stdlib-only, no pip deps), project-local venv via uv, argparse-based CLI entry point with subcommand groups: workspace, channel, catalog, backup, export, files, search. Each subcommand is a stub initially; ported from the existing scripts/*.sh + scripts/lib/*.sh one group at a time in follow-up issues. Goal: full replacement of the bash toolset per user decision (everything, stdlib argparse, project-local venv).
close_reason: pyproject.toml (stdlib-only, hatchling build), .venv via uv, src/slackbackup package, argparse CLI with 7 subcommand groups (workspace/channel/catalog/backup/export/files/search) all stubbed with NotImplementedError, 14 passing skeleton tests confirming subparser wiring and dispatch. Each group's real logic to be ported from the corresponding bash script(s) in follow-up issues.

title: export_transform.sh: jq ARG_MAX failure on months with many messages
description:
## Steps to Reproduce
Run scripts/export-monthly.sh against a real archive whose target month has enough
messages that the per-month transformed JSON exceeds the OS command-line length
limit, e.g.:
  ./scripts/export-monthly.sh --from 2026-01-01 --to 2026-06-22 \
    --workspace f3pugetsound --channel classifieds \
    --archive-root ~/slack-backups --out ~/slack-exports

## What happened
export_transform.sh line 134 passed the full per-month $messages JSON via
'jq --argjson', which exceeds the OS command-line length limit ('jq: Argument list
too long') once a month has enough messages. Hit in production on 3 of 15 tracked
channels (dungeons-of-finn-hill/ai-coding, f3cascades/all-f3-cascades,
f3pugetsound/classifieds) during the 2026-06-22 backup+export run.

Worse, bash's '> $target' redirect still created the output file before jq failed,
leaving a corrupt 0-byte file that a later idempotent re-run treated as
already-written and skipped (since the month was already sealed by the high-water
mark) — silently leaving permanently-missing data unless someone notices the
0-byte file.
design: Fixed by writing $messages to a temp file and passing it via 'jq --slurpfile messages <file>' (indexing $messages[0]) instead of --argjson on the command line. Re-ran scripts/test_export_monthly.sh (all pass) and re-exported the 3 affected channels after deleting their corrupt 2026-04 files.
acceptance_criteria: export-monthly.sh succeeds end-to-end on all 15 tracked channels with no zero-byte output files; scripts/test_export_monthly.sh passes.
close_reason: Fixed in scripts/lib/export_transform.sh: switched to --slurpfile via temp file instead of --argjson on the command line. Verified via scripts/test_export_monthly.sh (all pass) and a full re-export of all 15 tracked channels (47 output files, none empty).

title: Spike: evaluate slackdump native resume vs custom last_ts.txt logic
description: P1 design gap from review: scripts/incremental-dump.sh + last_ts.txt reimplements logic slackdump already provides natively (resuming a previous archive, adding only new messages). Determine whether slackdump's native resume mode satisfies UC-1 (incremental backup) and UC-2 (full re-sync ignoring prior state) as-is.
acceptance_criteria: Decision recorded: either (a) native resume satisfies UC-1/UC-2 and SlackBackup-4i2 is closed/superseded with scripts/bej/90o simplified to drop custom timestamp tracking, or (b) native resume is insufficient and the specific gap it leaves is documented as the justification for keeping custom logic.
close_reason: Native resume confirmed viable for UC-1/UC-2 (drop custom last_ts.txt logic in favor of archive+resume). But -dedupe flag has a confirmed data-loss bug: it deletes thread-root (IS_PARENT=1) message rows, verified by diffing a resume+dedupe archive against an independent fresh archive of the same channel (helpdesk, f3pugetsound workspace) - 9/9 thread parents missing. Decision: use resume WITHOUT -dedupe; accept duplicate rows across cycles rather than risk silent loss of thread metadata. Consider filing upstream with rusq/slackdump.

title: Python rewrite: backup/export/files list commands + help overview
description: Add symmetric read/list operations: backup list <channels.json> <archive-root> (per-channel archived status, message count, last-modified from local sqlite, no API call), export list <out-dir> [--workspace] [--channel] (scan exported <ws>-<ch>-yyyy-mm.json files), files list <index.json> (summary counts by workspace/mimetype from an index.json, independent of whether files fetch/index are ported yet). Also add slackbackup help: auto-generated overview of every <group> <command> pair with its one-line description, walking the argparse tree (no hand-maintained list to drift).
close_reason: Added backup list (local-only archived/message_count/last_modified per tracked channel), export list (scans <out>/<ws>-<ch>-yyyy-mm.json, resolves hyphenated workspace/channel names unambiguously when at least one filter is given - found and fixed a real ambiguous-split bug before it shipped), files list (summarize an index.json by workspace/mimetype/context_type, works independently of files fetch/index which are still unported), and slackbackup help (auto-generated from the argparse tree via introspection, can't drift out of sync). 12 new tests (84 total). Verified live: backup list against all 20 real tracked channels, export list against all 28 real exported files including filtered/unfiltered and hyphenated-name cases, help prints all 16 leaf commands.

title: search-messages.sh: cross-workspace message search to HTML report
description:
New capability, not part of the original DESIGN-files.md scope: search for
messages (not files) across every registered f3* workspace and render
results as one HTML page, most recent first, each linking to its channel
and to the message itself in Slack.

Implemented: scripts/search-messages.sh, scripts/lib/message_search_helpers.sh.
Reuses scripts/lib/fetch_files_helpers.sh's candidate_f3_workspaces/
is_workspace_registered (same f3*-workspace-loop pattern as fetch-files.sh).

SEARCH_MESSAGE already carries CHANNEL_ID, CHANNEL_NAME, TS, TXT, and the
DATA blob has a ready permalink - no channel-catalog lookup needed (unlike
build-file-index.sh's handling of `search files`' CHANNEL_ID="SEARCH"
placeholder).

Found and fixed two real HTML-escaping bugs against live data:
1. Double-escaping: Slack's TXT column already HTML-entity-escapes bare "&"
   (e.g. "PB&J") per Slack's documented formatting convention; naively
   applying jq's @html again produced "&amp;". Fixed by decoding
   </>/& back to raw characters first, then escaping once.
2. Unescaped mention/link syntax: Slack's own <@U..|name>/<#C..>/<url|label>
   syntax uses literal, unescaped angle brackets (that's syntax, not HTML) -
   embedding it raw risks the browser treating it as a bogus tag and
   silently dropping the content. Fixed by escaping the decoded text
   uniformly, so mentions render as safe literal text
   (e.g. "<@U123|name>") instead of vanishing or rendering unsafely.

12 passing tests (scripts/test_search_messages.sh), verified live against
all registered f3* workspaces (122 real results, correct sort order,
correct escaping, no unregistered-workspace failures).
close_reason: Implemented, tested (12 passing), and verified live against all registered f3* workspaces. See issue description for the two escaping bugs found and fixed.

title: build-file-index.sh: unify tracked-archive + search-result files into one index
description:
Implements the indexing half of docs/DESIGN-files.md.

Scope:
- new scripts/build-file-index.sh <out-files-dir> <index.json>
  [--archive-root <path>] [--search-root <path>]: two sqlite3 query passes
  (mimetype NOT LIKE 'image/%' against FILE) over (1) tracked-channel
  archives under the archive root, (2) SlackBackup-d02's search-result
  DB(s)/merged DB under the fetch-files output root.
- channel name resolution reads ONLY SlackBackup-6ql's catalog file — no
  fresh `slackdump list channels` call (binding constraint from 6ql).
- dedupe by file ID; first_seen/last_seen = min/max LOAD_DTTM across all
  matching rows from both sources.
- new sanitize_filename() helper, pure function, unit-testable.
- copy (never move) blobs from .../__uploads/<file-id>/<original-filename>
  to <out-files-dir>/<workspace>/<channel>/<filename>__<file-id>.<ext>.
- idempotent: skip copy+index-append for already-indexed IDs (wrote/skipped/
  empty stdout convention matching export_transform.sh); read-merge-write
  index.json via tmpfile+mv matching register-channel.sh's channels.json
  pattern.
- new scripts/test_build_file_index.sh: fully fixture-driven (hand-built
  slackdump.sqlite fixtures via sqlite3 < schema.sql, including an
  application/vnd.slack-docs canvas row and duplicate IDs across two fixture
  DBs), no live calls. Separate unit-test loop for sanitize_filename().

AC:
- Indexes both tracked-archive and search-harvested files, deduped by ID with
  correct first/last-seen.
- Channel names resolved via SlackBackup-6ql's catalog only.
- Idempotent re-run: no duplicate copies, no index corruption.
- Canvas files (application/vnd.slack-docs) included, verified via fixture.

Depends on: SlackBackup-6ql (catalog for name resolution),
SlackBackup-d02 (search-result DBs as second source).

See docs/DESIGN-files.md and plan at
/home/stuar/.claude/plans/explain-the-design-mcp-sleepy-moler.md
close_reason: Implemented: scripts/build-file-index.sh, scripts/lib/file_index_helpers.sh. FILE table has no MIMETYPE/etc columns -- non-image filtering and all metadata extraction is via json_extract against the DATA blob (raw Slack file JSON), confirmed empirically. search-result rows carry CHANNEL_ID='SEARCH' (a placeholder, also confirmed empirically); real channel id comes from DATA.channels[0] and is resolved via SlackBackup-6ql's catalog cache only (catalog_name_by_id, read-only, no API call), falling back to the raw id when uncataloged. message_context.type distinguishes true channel-level canvases (MESSAGE_ID NULL) from message-attached canvas-type files and plain search-only results with unknown message linkage. Dedup/aggregation (first_seen/last_seen min/max, earlier-source-wins on metadata) implemented as a pure jq function. 21 passing tests (9 pure-logic + 12 full fixture-driven end-to-end incl. idempotent re-run) in scripts/test_build_file_index.sh. Verified live against all real tracked-channel archives in ~/slack-backups: correctly indexed videos/zips/pptx/html/canvases, zero images leaked through, one bad-data edge case (no filename) handled gracefully without crashing.

title: fetch-files.sh: search-files harvesting across registered f3* workspaces
description:
Implements the file-harvesting half of docs/DESIGN-files.md (search-files
side; pairs with build-file-index.sh for the indexing side).

Scope:
- new scripts/fetch-files.sh <out-archive-root> [--terms-file <path>]: loops
  only currently-registered f3* workspaces (check against `slackdump
  workspace list`); warns+skips (doesn't fail) any unregistered f3*
  workspace, naming register-workspace.sh as the remedy. Per workspace, per
  search term: `slackdump search files <term>` into
  <out-archive-root>/<workspace>/<term>/, then merge into one per-workspace
  DB.
- search terms externalized to scripts/config/file-search-terms.txt (one per
  line, #-comments), default list: pax, convergence, help, ruck, ao, health.
- new scripts/lib/fetch_files_helpers.sh: pure-logic pieces (term-file
  parsing, registered-workspace filtering) split out for unit testing without
  invoking slackdump.
- new scripts/test_fetch_files.sh: live/manual, workspace-arg-gated (not
  automated CI), plus unit tests of the helpers lib against fixtures.
- Not wired into run-backups.sh — manual/occasional invocation only.

BLOCKING PRECONDITION: empirically verify `slackdump tools merge` semantics
(idempotent? additive? same-workspace-only?) before depending on it for the
per-workspace merge step. Record findings as an addendum to
docs/references/slackdump-cli-notes.md's "## tools merge" section. Documented
fallback if merge proves unsuitable: keep per-term result DBs separate and
have build-file-index.sh (SlackBackup-<C-issue-id>) read across all of them
directly instead of one merged DB.

AC:
- Unregistered f3* workspaces skipped with a warning, not a failure.
- Term list lives in editable config file, not hardcoded in the script.
- tools merge semantics verified+documented before being relied on; fallback
  documented if unsuitable.
- Never invoked from run-backups.sh.

Depends on: SlackBackup-6ql (workspace-iteration conventions land there
first).

See docs/DESIGN-files.md and plan at
/home/stuar/.claude/plans/explain-the-design-mcp-sleepy-moler.md
close_reason: Implemented: scripts/fetch-files.sh, scripts/lib/fetch_files_helpers.sh, scripts/config/file-search-terms.txt. Blocking precondition resolved empirically: slackdump tools merge cannot combine search-result DBs (empty CHANNEL table -> 'getting channels: no data found'); documented in docs/references/slackdump-cli-notes.md. Adopted documented fallback: per-term result dirs kept separate, no merge step, build-file-index.sh (SlackBackup-t2b) reads across them directly. 6 passing unit tests (scripts/test_fetch_files.sh). Verified live against real f3* workspaces: registered workspaces harvested, unregistered ones skipped with correct register-workspace.sh remedy, idempotent re-run produces all skipped/no new API calls. Not wired into run-backups.sh.

title: Channel catalog: generalize register-channel.sh into fast/full-tier catalog (single source of truth)
description:
Implements the channel-catalog half of docs/DESIGN-files.md.

Binding design constraint: ONE catalog data file per workspace
($CACHE_DIR/<ws>.catalog.tsv, replacing the existing <ws>.txt), columns id,
name, member(yes/no), description. Fast tier (`list channels -member-only
-format JSON`) upserts member=yes rows; full tier (`list channels -format
JSON`, explicit/manual only) upserts remaining IDs as member=no, never
clobbering an existing member=yes row. Both tiers parse JSON output (not the
default Text table) so `topic`/`purpose` -> `description` come from the same
single API call, no extra query. No script outside the catalog-refresh
functions may call `slackdump list channels` directly.

Scope:
- scripts/register-channel.sh: rename cache file, add member+description
  columns, add get_full_channel_list(), switch list parsing to JSON+jq, wire
  full-tier fallback into existing name-lookup on a fast-tier miss.
- new scripts/catalog-channels.sh <workspace> [--full]: prints the catalog
  dump (id, name, member, description, tracked, last_posted-for-tracked).
- last-posted: local-only `SELECT MAX(ts) FROM MESSAGE` per tracked channel's
  slackdump.sqlite, no API call.
- run-backups.sh: one added call to warm the fast-tier cache per workspace
  touched, fast tier only, never full.
- delete stale channels-T*.txt (4 files in repo root) once verified replaced.
- new scripts/test_catalog_channels.sh (fixture-driven, stubbed list
  functions, asserts member-flag-never-downgraded + description parsing).

AC:
- Single catalog file per workspace; fast+full tiers both write into it.
- Full tier never sets member=no over an existing member=yes row.
- description populated from topic (fallback purpose) via -format JSON, same
  call, no extra query.
- No script outside catalog-refresh functions calls `slackdump list
  channels` directly (grep -rn "list channels" scripts/ confirms this).
- register-channel.sh name lookup falls back to full-tier refresh (not an
  inline list channels call) on a fast-tier miss.
- run-backups.sh warms only the fast tier, automatically, once per run.
- Stale channels-T*.txt files removed.

See docs/DESIGN-files.md and plan at
/home/stuar/.claude/plans/explain-the-design-mcp-sleepy-moler.md
close_reason: Implemented: scripts/lib/channel_catalog.sh (single catalog file/workspace, fast+full tiers, description via -format JSON same-call, no script outside this lib calls list channels directly), scripts/catalog-channels.sh, register-channel.sh refactored onto it, run-backups.sh fast-tier warm-up, stale channels-T*.txt removed, scripts/test_catalog_channels.sh (12 passing unit tests). Verified live: catalog refresh, name-based registration via fallback, tracked/last_posted cross-ref against real archives.

title: export_transform.sh: noise reduction + display_name resolution + late-reply-to-sealed-month fix
description:
## What changed
Cleaned up the monthly export schema for LLM-driven analysis of conversations/threads/
links/references:
- Reduced each message (parent and reply alike) to ts/user/display_name/text/files,
  dropping Slack-API plumbing (blocks, client_msg_id, team, user_profile, metadata,
  reply_count, reply_users, replace_original, etc.).
- Resolved `user` (id, kept for cross-referencing) to a new `display_name` field using
  the `users.json` that `slackdump convert -f export` writes alongside the day files
  (profile.display_name -> real_name -> name -> raw id fallback chain).
- Handled bot/system messages that have no `user` field at all (use `bot_id`/`username`
  instead) - this crashed jq with "Cannot index object with null" on a real archive
  (f3cascades/otb-red-eye-reps) before the fix.
- Fixed a related correctness gap surfaced while discussing the cross-month-thread
  design with the user: sealing a month only means "no new threads can start there",
  not "no new replies can land there" - Slack threads can get late replies long after
  their parent's month was sealed and written. The skip-on-sealed logic now also
  requires the freshly computed content to match what's on disk; a late reply now
  reopens and rewrites the sealed month (logged as "rewrote (late reply to sealed
  month)") instead of being silently dropped forever.
design: See docs/DESIGN-export.md (Data Model + Crosscutting Concepts >> Idempotency sections, updated). Implementation in scripts/lib/export_transform.sh.
acceptance_criteria: scripts/test_export_monthly.sh passes (13 cases incl. display_name resolution/fallback, bot-message handling, files reduction, noise stripping, and the late-reply-reopens-sealed-month scenario). Full re-export of all 15 tracked channels succeeds with no failures and no zero-byte files.
close_reason: Implemented in scripts/lib/export_transform.sh + scripts/test_export_monthly.sh + scripts/test_fixtures/export-archive/users.json, design notes in docs/DESIGN-export.md. Verified via test suite (13 passing cases) and a full re-export of all 15 real tracked channels.

title: Implement scripts/export-monthly.sh monthly JSON exporter
description: Implement the exporter designed in docs/DESIGN-export.md: read-only consumer of slackdump.sqlite that runs 'slackdump convert -f export', then transforms day-files into per-month <ws>-<slug>-yyyy-mm.json with replies nested under their parent. Range bounds and month-bucketing key on the parent ts; threads stay whole in their parent's month. Idempotent: skip sealed months whose file exists, always rewrite the trailing (high-water) month. Seal predicate: .last_backup stamp if present else message high-water mark.
design: Strategy B (read via documented export boundary). jq transform; GNU date for month math; sealed iff later-month data exists OR .last_backup>end_of_month.
acceptance_criteria: 1) 1-root/2-reply thread -> one top-level msg with replies[] in ts order, no reply at top level. 2) cross-month thread lands wholly in parent's month file. 3) --from/--to bounds threads by parent ts. 4) 3-month archive -> 3 correctly-named files. 5) second run skips existing sealed months, bytes unchanged. 6) filename exactly <ws>-<slug>-yyyy-mm.json. 7) exit 2 when archive missing.
notes: Implemented: scripts/export-monthly.sh (entry point, I4 contract, exit 2 on missing archive), scripts/lib/export_transform.sh (range bound by parent ts, month bucketing, thread nesting, sealed-month idempotency with .last_backup stamp + high-water fallback). Fixtures in scripts/test_fixtures/export-archive/; tests in scripts/test_export_monthly.sh (test-plan items 1-6 + seal-stamp). Boundary (item 7) validated against real 'slackdump convert -f export' output: layout and verbatim field preservation confirmed. CONTEXT.md Core Capabilities + Non-Goals scope note updated. Seal-stamp write in backup.sh tracked by SlackBackup-026.
close_reason: Exporter implemented and tested; all test-plan ACs pass, real-boundary round-trip validated.

title: backup.sh: write durable .last_backup UTC stamp after successful archive/resume
description: The monthly exporter (docs/DESIGN-export.md) seals a month as complete when last_backup > end-of-month. backup.sh must write a git-durable <channel-dir>/.last_backup (UTC, file content not mtime) after each successful archive/resume. Without it, the exporter falls back to the message high-water mark, which rewrites a quiet channel's last active month on every run.

title: Design: monthly JSON exporter (workspace-channel-yyyy-mm.json, nested threads, idempotent)
description: Design a tool that reads the slackdump.sqlite archives and exports a bounded date range to per-month JSON files named workspace-channel-yyyy-mm.json, with thread replies nested under their parent, skipping any output file that already exists. Captured in docs/DESIGN-export.md.
close_reason: Design captured in docs/DESIGN-export.md

title: Add register-channel.sh and workspace-aware channels.json schema
description: channels.json currently has no workspace field, so backup.sh/run-backups.sh rely on whatever workspace slackdump currently has selected -- wrong for a multi-workspace channel list. Add a workspace field to the schema, a register-channel.sh helper (lookup channel ID by name within a workspace, append entry), and make backup.sh select the right workspace before archiving each channel.
acceptance_criteria: register-channel.sh <workspace> <channel-name-or-id> appends a {id,name,workspace} entry to channels.json (creating it if absent, no duplicates). validate-channels.sh requires workspace field. run-backups.sh selects the correct workspace per channel before calling backup.sh, validated against channels spanning both f3pugetsound and f3kirkland.

title: Port proven local backup into GitHub Actions workflow
description: Once SlackBackup-90o (and the channels.json/incremental logic it depends on) is working as a local script invoked manually or via local cron, wrap the same scripts/ in .github/workflows/backup.yml as the second trigger mode. No new backup logic here — only the GitHub Actions trigger, secrets-as-env-vars wiring, and the clone/commit/push step.
acceptance_criteria: Workflow triggers on schedule and workflow_dispatch, invokes the same scripts/ already validated locally, and produces identical output to a local run for the same channel/time range.
close_reason: GitHub Actions automation is off the table: workspace registration requires a fresh xoxd- cookie pasted manually each time (deliberately never persisted, since it's short-lived/sensitive), which is fundamentally incompatible with an unattended scheduled CI job. Project direction is local CLI (./slackbackup) only.

title: Write fork-ready README with slackdump view commands
Modularize to scripts/
close_reason: Delivered as part of SlackBackup-90o: scripts/backup.sh (core trigger-agnostic logic) and scripts/install-slackdump.sh now exist under scripts/.

title: Add incremental TS logic
notes: Blocked on SlackBackup-d3r spike result — may be closed/superseded rather than implemented if native slackdump resume mode covers UC-1/UC-2.
close_reason: Superseded by SlackBackup-d3r spike result: slackdump's native 'resume' (without -dedupe, due to confirmed thread-root data-loss bug) covers UC-1/UC-2 incremental and full-resync needs directly. No custom last_ts.txt/calc_ts_range logic needed.

title: Extract channel config to channels.json and loop
description: Multi-channel support: channels.json (array of {id, name}) declares which channels to back up; a loop script invokes backup.sh per channel instead of the single hardcoded channel.
acceptance_criteria: channels.json with N channel entries results in N archive directories being created/updated when the loop script runs. Malformed/missing channels.json fails loudly, not silently.
close_reason: channels.json + scripts/run-backups.sh (loop) + scripts/validate-channels.sh (fail-loud schema check) delivered and tested against real channels in f3pugetsound.

title: Implement MVP workflow — single hardcoded channel
description: MVP: a script that backs up one hardcoded channel (#helpdesk / CJBG619C5, f3pugetsound workspace) locally — no GitHub Actions yet (see SlackBackup-dj8).
acceptance_criteria: Running scripts/backup.sh with no existing archive runs 'slackdump archive' for the hardcoded channel into a local archive dir. Running it again when an archive already exists runs 'slackdump resume' (without -dedupe, per SlackBackup-d3r finding) instead of re-archiving from scratch. Running it twice in a row produces no duplicate or missing messages (spot-checked via message count / ts diff).
notes: Slice implemented and validated (scripts/backup.sh + install-slackdump.sh, real #helpdesk channel, zero data loss confirmed). Leaving open per I11 — bd correctly blocks close on the still-open hardening tests (jcf/4a8/03m/c86); closing happens once those land, not now.
close_reason: All hardening tests landed (jcf, 4a8, 03m, c86) against real channels. MVP complete: scripts/backup.sh + install-slackdump.sh, validated for zero data loss on archive/resume cycles.

title: Write unit tests for scripts/ (red phase) — JSON parse + TS calc
close_reason: scripts/validate-channels.sh extracted as a standalone, deterministic unit (no slackdump/Slack boundary, T12) and scripts/test_validate_channels.sh covers 9 cases (valid single/multi, empty array, wrong type, missing/empty id/name, malformed JSON, missing file) — all passing. TS-calc portion of original scope dropped (SlackBackup-4i2 superseded).

title: Write E2E acceptance tests (red phase) — UC-3 multi-channel
close_reason: scripts/test_multichannel.sh: real channels.json with a bad + good channel ID confirms the good channel is still archived (25 msgs) regardless of the preceding failure. Surfaced a real gap (tracked separately): slackdump exits 0 and writes a silent 0-message archive for a not-found channel rather than failing loudly.

title: Write E2E acceptance tests (red phase) — UC-2 full resync
close_reason: backup.sh -f flag + scripts/test_full_resync.sh: full re-sync writes to a fresh timestamped directory rather than re-running archive against an existing one (which was found to duplicate data, not overwrite). Confirmed incremental archive untouched (sha256 unchanged) and full archive complete (55 msgs).

title: Write E2E acceptance tests (red phase) — UC-1 incremental backup
close_reason: scripts/test_incremental.sh formalizes the archive-then-resume idempotency check against the real #helpdesk channel (real boundary, no stub, per T14). Passing.

title: all-pax (f3pugetsound) archives with 0 messages despite 1830 members
description: slackdump archive -o <dir> C77PPNCUT completes successfully (chunk #10, type MESSAGES, final=1) but with num_rec=0 - the Slack API returned zero messages for this channel, confirmed reproducible (re-archived from scratch twice with identical result). Not a corruption/bug in the SlackBackup tooling (bash or Python) - the archive command itself reports success with a complete, empty MESSAGES chunk. all-pax is the workspace's renamed 'general' channel (previous_names: ['general'], is_general: true), posting restricted to admins per its channel purpose. Hypothesis: a permissions/scope issue specific to this channel for the current session token, not a tooling bug. Needs investigation with a fresh session cookie or by checking Slack admin-side channel history settings - not actionable from this codebase alone.
